itemListElement.name: Jaguar XJ X351 5.0 V8 / XJR / XJR575
Jaguar F-Type X152 P450 / R / SVR

mainEntity.name: How much does an AJ133 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ133 typically costs £5,500–£9,000 supply only [JAG-QUOTE], with NA and supercharged variants priced similarly. Fitting adds £910–£1,710 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the AJ133 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with a caveat — it carries a 3.5-star rating from 330 UK enquiries [JAG-VERIFIED]. Pre-2013 units have a documented chain-tensioner risk; the design was revised in 2013 with a marked improvement.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common AJ133 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear on pre-2013 units, typically surfacing as a rattle on cold start between 60,000 and 100,000 miles if left unaddressed.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar models use the AJ133?

acceptedAnswer.text: The XF, XJ, F-Type and XK all use the AJ133 across various states of tune, including the XFR, XJR and F-Type R/SVR performance variants.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar AJ133 the same as Land Rover's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — it's the identical physical engine, badged AJ133 on both sides. Reliability, chain-tensioner risk, and parts cost are the same; only fitment and labour access differ slightly by model.

hero.h1: Jaguar AJ133 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar AJ133 is a 5.0-litre supercharged V8 — reliable, but pre-2013 units need chain-tensioner history. Backed by 330+ enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: 88% confidence — based on 330 UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist rebuild reports.
verdictRating.scoreNote: Engine Score breakdown (inherited unchanged from the Land Rover AJ133 page — same physical engine):

rows.score: 8 (fixed table: £7,250 recon midpoint falls in the £7,000–£9,999 band)
12 (editorial anchor: a mix of cheap fixes — chain tensioner — and a few expensive ones — supercharger/internals)
20 (editorial anchor: well-supported V8, widely available from multiple UK suppliers)
4 (fixed table: £7,250 recon against a conservative low-end host value exceeds the 75%+ band)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The AJ133 is a dependable, well-supported V8 — pre-2013 units carry a real chain-tensioner risk that has since been resolved — buy post-2013 or demand full history.
verdictRating.bestFor: buyers of higher-value XJ and F-Type V8s.
verdictRating.avoidIf: the host car's value can't absorb a £7,000+ recon.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Land Rover Range Rover, Range Rover Sport, Discovery 4 and Defender → landroverengine.uk/engines/aj133/

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an AJ133 typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Jaguar and Land Rover applications — both draw from the same £5,500–£9,000 recon range; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access, particularly on the F-Type's tighter transverse-adjacent packaging.
